translations.en: O Muhammad ibn Abu Bakr, know that the best instruction in religion is piety in the practice of religion and acting in His obedience. I exhort you to be God-fearing in your private and public matters, in any situation. This world is a place of test and tribulation, the hereafter is an abode of reward and eternity. So act for the abode which is forever, turn away from what will perish, but do not forget your portion from the bounties of this world. I enjoin you seven virtues that represent complete Islam: Fear Allah and do not fear people in His presence; The best speech is that confirmed by conduct; Do not give two contradicting judgements in one matter lest your directive become discrepant and you deviate from truth; Love for your subjects what you love for yourself and your family, and detest for them what you detest for yourself, for that establishes authority and benefits the people; Fight in the way of truth and do not fear reproach in the way of Allah; Give good advice when consulted; Make yourself an ideal for Muslims near and far.
